message:
fix(http): correct Content-Length handling and idle timeout for HTTP/1.1

Three protocol correctness fixes:

Client: Empty-body POST/PUT requests now emit Content-Length: 0 as
required by HTTP/1.1. Previously only non-empty bodies got the header.

Codec: 1xx, 204, and 304 responses no longer emit Content-Length.
RFC 7230 says these status codes MUST NOT include a message body, so
Content-Length is inappropriate. Update 204 response test to assert
absence of Content-Length.

Server: Apply idle timeout starting from the first request. Previously,
the requests_served > 0 gate meant the first request could block the
connection indefinitely if the client was slow to send data. Remove the
gate from both read_next and serve_connection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
